title: Typed Records, Missingness, Provenance, Validation, and Rejection
description: The scraper emits loose dictionaries, converts missing prices to zero, drops source locations, and keeps partly invalid rows because most fields look useful. Learners cumulatively produce typed provenance-preserving extraction record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model raw, parsed, normalized, validated, and exported fields with dataclasses or typed records and a versioned schema.
Keep missing, empty, unknown, redacted, malformed, not-applicable, and zero values distinct through validation and export.
Attach source URL, response identity, selector or rule, node evidence, retrieval time, parser, and transformation history to decisions.
